copernicus_soil_water.label: Copernicus satellite soil-water proxy
limitations: Regional proxy, satellite/model product, not an in-situ peat water-table sensor.
Copernicus SWI is emitted as an independent proxy and is not used to derive peat water-table depth.
Hydrological pressure classes are not derived from SWI at this stage.
Raw valid values are 0..200 and decoded with scale_factor 0.5 to percent; fill value 255 and flags 241, 242, 251, 252, 253 and 254 are unavailable, never converted to zero.
copernicus_soil_water.product: Soil Water Index 2025-present (raster 1 km), Europe, daily – version 2
copernicus_soil_water.selection_method: Deterministically select the nearest/containing EPSG:4326 1/112° Copernicus SWI grid cell to Wiesmoor (53.4167, 7.7333); no averaging, no conversion to peat water-table depth.

source.access_method: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem via Copernicus Browser, OData API, or S3; this adapter does not guess collection identifiers or file paths without verified anonymous discovery.
source.dataset: Soil Water Index 2025-present (raster 1 km), Europe, daily – version 2
source.name: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service

adapter_errors: Live Copernicus SWI discovery/download is not enabled: anonymous CDSE product discovery was not verified in this environment, and the adapter must not guess OData collection identifiers or file paths.

groundwater_proxy.interpretation_note: Nearby groundwater stations can indicate regional groundwater behaviour but are not an in-situ peat water-table sensor for Wiesmoor-Nord.
groundwater_proxy.label: groundwater proxy
groundwater_proxy.selection_method: Fetch official NLWKN Grundwasserstandonline public station metadata, parse station coordinates and current values when present, then select the nearest suitable stations to Wiesmoor (53.4167, 7.7333) by haversine distance; prefer stations with live value/date/status metadata but never fabricate missing values.

source.dataset: Grundwasserstandonline public station metadata with current daily value where exposed
source.name: NLWKN Grundwasserstandonline / groundwater monitoring data

peat_context.context_note: Wiesmoor is a settlement and landscape shaped by raised-bog peat extraction, drainage, peat-fired industry and subsequent agricultural/horticultural use. MoorIS Niedersachsen maps the Wiesmoor-Nord peatland context, including peat-thickness information; this observer carries that context as static source metadata rather than as a live sensor.

peat_thickness_context.description: MoorIS Niedersachsen / Wiesmoor-Nord provides local peat-thickness context for the mapped peatland area.
peat_thickness_context.precision_note: No numeric peat-thickness value is emitted until a reproducible MoorIS data extraction is added.

limitations: Regional proxy observation — not an in-situ peat water-table sensor.
DWD daily rainfall is one regional proxy component only and is not an in-situ peat water-table measurement.
NLWKN groundwater stations are a regional proxy; station distance and local hydrogeological representativeness are uncertain.
Groundwater level observations are not the same as a peat water-table measurement in Wiesmoor-Nord peat soils.
Groundwater stations, satellite soil-water data and weather totals may describe different depths, footprints and response times.

regional_soil_water.dataset: Daily grids of mean soil moisture under grass for Germany, Version v1.0
regional_soil_water.label: regional DWD gridded soil-moisture condition
limitations: Regional gridded model product, not an in-situ peat water-table sensor.
DWD AMBAV soil moisture under grass preserves source-native ‰ nFK semantics and is not converted to water-table depth.
regional_soil_water.selection_method: Deterministically select the source grid cell containing or nearest to Wiesmoor (53.4167, 7.7333) from the DWD NetCDF grid metadata; no regional averaging and no conversion to peat water-table depth.

source.dataset: Daily grids of mean soil moisture under grass for Germany, Version v1.0
source.name: Deutscher Wetterdienst (DWD) Climate Data Center

regional_soil_water.variable: mean soil moisture under grass, 0-60 cm layer

sources.dataset: Grundwasserstandonline public station metadata with current daily value where exposed
sources.name: NLWKN Grundwasserstandonline / groundwater monitoring data

sources.dataset: Daily grids of mean soil moisture under grass for Germany, Version v1.0
sources.name: Deutscher Wetterdienst (DWD) Climate Data Center

sources.access_method: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem via Copernicus Browser, OData API, or S3; this adapter does not guess collection identifiers or file paths without verified anonymous discovery.
sources.dataset: Soil Water Index 2025-present (raster 1 km), Europe, daily – version 2
sources.name: Copernicus Land Monitoring Service

sources.dataset: Recent daily climate observations Germany (KL), daily precipitation RSK and daily mean temperature TMK
sources.name: Deutscher Wetterdienst (DWD) Climate Data Center

summary: Wiesmoor peatland hydrological pressure remains unavailable as an in-situ conclusion; DWD daily rainfall is emitted when available as a regional weather proxy component only.

coverage.dry_day_rule: A dry day is a valid DWD daily precipitation (RSK) observation below 1.0 mm/day; exactly 1.0 mm/day is not dry. Missing markers and absent dates are unavailable, never dry.
coverage.minimum_coverage_rule: 7-day total requires 7/7 valid precipitation days; 30-day total requires at least 27/30 valid precipitation days. DWD -999/missing and absent dates are unavailable, not zero.

weather_pressure.label: weather pressure

source.dataset: Recent daily climate observations Germany (KL), daily precipitation RSK and daily mean temperature TMK
source.name: Deutscher Wetterdienst (DWD) Climate Data Center

station.selection_method: Nearest DWD CDC recent daily climate station to Wiesmoor (53.4167, 7.7333) with station metadata end date within 14 days; distance uses haversine great-circle calculation.

weather_pressure.temperature_label: latest valid daily mean temperature (TMK), not an instantaneous/current temperature
